relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains loan application data with applicant, employment, financial, and collateral details. The outbound file is a transaction hold notice targeting specific high-risk transactions with country-based sanctions screening. While both files pass through Risk and Compliance, they serve fundamentally different purposes: one is intake/assessment of loan applications, the other is enforcement action on transactions. The outbound file's transaction-level data (transactionId, destinationCountry, transaction amounts) and sanctions-based hold logic have no corresponding fields in the loan application schema. The only minor overlap is customer identifiers and timestamps, which are too generic to establish meaningful lineage.
